title: Tax Considerations When Caring for a Parent
summary: Caregiving can come with real tax implications worth understanding, even if taxes feel like the last thing on your mind.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Tax Considerations When Caring for a Parent | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Caregiving can come with real tax implications worth understanding, even if taxes feel like the last thing on your mind.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Some caregiving-related medical expenses may be tax-deductible.
Claiming a parent as a dependent has specific income and support requirements.
A tax professional can often find savings families don't realize exist.

paragraphs: Certain unreimbursed medical expenses paid on a parent's behalf — including some home modifications and long-term care costs — may be deductible if you itemize and expenses exceed a certain threshold of your income.

Claiming a parent as a dependent has specific requirements around the parent's income and how much financial support you actually provided — rules that catch many caregivers by surprise, in both directions.

nextSteps: Keep organized records of caregiving-related expenses throughout the year.
Ask a tax professional specifically about dependent eligibility for your situation.
Don't assume eligibility either way without checking current rules.
disclaimer: General information, not tax advice — a tax professional can confirm what applies to your specific situation and the current tax year.
